nuggets.headline: From Metalcore Band 'In Alcatraz 1962' to Producing for Anderson .Paak
nuggets.text: Before crafting beats, Pete Rango's musical foundation was in a high school metal/hardcore band called In Alcatraz 1962. That experience was the direct catalyst for his obsession with production, pushing him to learn how to record, mix, and master his own records. This evolution from live band percussion to in-the-box production defines his hands-on, multi-genre approach, which eventually led to him creating demos with artists like Anderson .Paak.

source.title: Meet Pete Rango - Voyage MIA Magazine | Miami City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: I was in a metal/hardcore band in high school called In Alcatraz 1962... That was the catalyst for me wanting to learn how to record, mix, and produce my own records.

nuggets.imageCaption: Pete Rango in the studio, reflecting his hands-on production approach.

nuggets.headline: The Philosophy Driving The Beat: Disrupting The System with XUE Records
nuggets.text: This track is more than just a beat; it's a product of Rango's core mission to "disrupt, uplift, and make the system nervous." He founded the nonprofit L.I.V. Corp and his own label, XUE Records, to build a world where community and technology collide, completely outside the traditional industry machine. As you listen, hear the arrangement as a manifestation of that DIY ethos — a self-contained world built from the ground up.

source.title: rush | PETE RANGO
source.quoteSnippet: A Colombian Artist, Producer and Culture-Builder... His mission: Disrupt. Uplift. And make the System nervous.

nuggets.imageCaption: Oms at Play by Pete Rango

nuggets.headline: If 'Oms at Play' Resonates, You Need Samiyam's 'Animals Have Feelings'
nuggets.text: The off-kilter rhythm and textural grit of this track are direct descendants of the L.A. beat scene. For a deeper cut into that sound, get into Ann Arbor producer Samiyam. His 2016 album for Stones Throw, "Animals Have Feelings," is a masterclass in the kind of warped, video-game-influenced instrumental hip-hop that clearly informs Rango's work on "Savage Planet."

source.title: Meet Pete Rango - Voyage MIA Magazine | Miami City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: During college, I started discovering electronic music. I was on my Grizzly Bear, Animal Collective shit, and then I started getting into mashups and mixing different genres.

nuggets.imageCaption: Producer Samiyam, a key figure in the experimental beat scene.

artistSummary: Bogota-born, Virginia-based producer Pete Rango is a culture-builder with a DIY ethos. His sound pulls from hardcore, hip-hop, and electronic music, driven by a mission to disrupt the system through his label XUE Records.
